search_queries_used: site:cpoc.org.uk surgery opioids postoperative pain service review follow-up
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic management or dose instructions for Post-operative pain service review
generic anesthesia landing pages without an exact section for Post-operative pain service review
rejection_reasons: Controlled medicines, doses, conversion, and escalation require direct pain-team judgment.
The candidate source category "automatic analgesic escalation ladders" did not provide exact documentation-only evidence for this workflow and was not mapped.
Autonomous management, medication, dose, procedure, escalation, or discharge instructions were outside the documentation-only scope.
Landing pages and search snippets were not accepted as exact evidence.
population_applicability: Postoperative patients with pain requiring functional assessment or pain-service review, including complex pain or prior opioid use.
setting_applicability: Direct for documenting an already performed or arranged pain-service review; treatment and prescribing remain outside scope.
UAE_applicability: International CPOC evidence requires UAE pain-service, controlled-medicine, and facility pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Official CPOC 2021 PDF and current ASA and RCoA sources were reviewed on 2026-07-13; CPOC scheduled review is overdue.
superseded_check: No newer CPOC surgery-and-opioids document was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact CPOC section supports functional pain assessment, complex pain or pain-service review, discharge communication, and clinician-arranged follow-up without generating analgesic treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports functional pain assessment, complex pain or pain-service review, discharge communication, and clinician-arranged follow-up without autonomous treatment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact ASA preanesthesia documentation section supports risk, airway, cardiorespiratory, reviewed-investigation, consultation, plan, and consent fields for Post-operative pain service review; each remains clinician-entered and conditional on assessment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.

unresolved_source_gaps: Pain diagnosis, location, mechanism, score, function, medicine, dose, opioid use, side effects, intervention, response, discharge prescription, and follow-up timing remain unsupported.
Legacy duration presets, broad generic interval-history wording, and any content not explicitly mapped to an exact section remain unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No source mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, anesthesia plan, investigation, procedure, medication, dose, referral, discharge decision, or urgency conclusion.
UAE facility policy, credentialing, pathway, and qualified anesthesiologist review remain required before use.

legacy_item_support_mappings.direct_relationship: The exact CPOC section supports functional pain assessment, complex pain or pain-service review, discharge communication, and clinician-arranged follow-up without generating analgesic treatment.
